question: Let ABCDEF be a regular hexagon with center I and circumradius 8. Point Z is the midpoint of segment BC. Let N be the rotation of point Z by angle 5pi/12 around point D. Let W be the rotation of point B by angle pi/6 around point C. Let V be the reflection of point W across point E. Point M is the midpoint of segment AC. Point H is obtained by rotating point M by angle 11pi/12 about point N. Find the length of segment VH. Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

Let ABCDEF be a regular hexagon with center Q and circumradius 5. Line H passes through points A and E. Let U be the reflection of line H through point Q. Let I be the rotation of point Q by angle 7pi/12 around point B. Point V is the reflection of point I across line H. Let M be the reflection of line U through point V. Let G be the line through point D perpendicular to line M. Point Y is the reflection of point V across line G. What is the length of segment YD? Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

ABCDEFG is a regular 7-gon with center O and circumradius 11. Let K be the midpoint of segment AD. Point Y is the midpoint of segment AB. Point P is obtained by rotating point K by angle pi/4 about point F. Point X is the midpoint of segment CP. Find the length of segment XY. Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.

Let ABCDEFGHIJK be a regular 11-gon with center Y and circumradius 4. Let W be the vector from point J to point C. Let U be the rotation of point G by angle 7pi/12 around point J. Line Q is the angle bisector of angle AUG. Point L is the reflection of point H across line Q. Let Z be point B translated by vector W. Line R is the angle bisector of angle LBC. Point P is the reflection of point Z across line R. What is the length of segment PY? Express your answer as a decimal number rounded to 2 decimal places.
